brief: Release broadcast summarizes the new GitHub-native intake flow, shared runtime extraction, and the demo-data seeding pass across the Pages fleet.

outputNotes.narrated bulletin: A narrated version helps teams absorb the release quickly without reading the whole changelog.
outputNotes.HTML release page: The release page is the fastest public-facing summary to ship.
outputNotes.RSS feed: The feed matters when you want the same release artifact to fan out to subscribers and downstream tools.
outputNotes.scripted brief: The scripted brief is the shared release source that all the other surfaces branch from.

searchSummary: GitHub-native intake, safer launch paths, seeded Pages demo rollout.
whyItMatters: This release is part of a family. Search makes it easier to spot repeated themes like intake safety, multi-surface publishing, and runtime consolidation across multiple releases.
searchIntro: Search across the release set to see how one artifact family turns into a running product narrative over time.

brief: This earlier release introduced the shared runtime contract and cut down per-app workflow duplication so Pages apps could publish from the same Bonfyre path.
searchSummary: Shared runtime contract, workflow reduction, single publish path.
whyItMatters: Together with the April release, this shows that release search is not just about one post. It lets teams compare platform changes across time.

brief: This release seeded first-visit demo data across the Pages fleet so every app showed a working flow before users uploaded their own material.
searchSummary: First-visit demo data, onboarding, seeded Pages experience.
whyItMatters: Release search becomes useful when users can pull together related changes like onboarding, runtime work, and publishing from one set of artifacts.
